lines: Class 1 Maths – All Terms with Simple Formulas & Examples (CBSE)
Class 10 Maths – All Terms with Formulas & Examples (CBSE)
Euclid’s Division Lemma → a = bq + rExample → 17 = 5 × 3 + 2
HCF (Euclid’s Algorithm)HCF(a, b) = HCF(b, r)Example → HCF(26, 12) = 2
Zeros RelationSum = −b/aProduct = c/aExample → x² − 5x + 6 → Sum = 5, Product = 6
Form → a₁x + b₁y + c₁ = 0a₂x + b₂y + c₂ = 0Example → x + y = 5, x − y = 1
nth Term → aₙ = a + (n−1)dExample → a=2, d=3, n=4 → a₄ = 11
sinθ = P/H, cosθ = B/H, tanθ = P/BExample → sin30° = 1/2
n(A ∪ B) = n(A) + n(B) − n(A ∩ B)Example → 10 + 8 − 3 = 15
sinθ = P/H, cosθ = B/H, tanθ = P/BExample → sin30° = 1/2
(a + b)ⁿ = Σ [ⁿCᵣ aⁿ⁻ʳ bʳ]Example → (a + b)² = a² + 2ab + b²
If p → qExample → If x>0 → x²>0
Composite Function → (f ∘ g)(x) = f(g(x))Example → f(x)=2x, g(x)=x+1 → f(g(2)) = 6
Inverse Function → f(f⁻¹(x)) = xExample → f(x)=2x → f⁻¹(x)=x/2
sin⁻¹x + cos⁻¹x = π/2Example → sin⁻¹(1/2) + cos⁻¹(1/2) = π/2
lim x→a f(x) = f(a)Example → f(x)=x² at x=2 → 4
Max/Min → f′(x) = 0Example → x² → min at x=0
∫ₐᵇ f(x) dx = F(b) − F(a)Example → ∫₀¹ x dx = 1/2
dy/dx = f(x)Example → dy/dx = x → y = x²/2 + C
Objective Function → Z = ax + byExample → Max Z = 2x + 3y
Class 2 Maths – All Terms with Formulas & Examples (CBSE)
Expanded Form → Sum of place valuesExample → 345 = 300 + 40 + 5
Class 3 Maths – All Terms with Formulas & Examples (CBSE)
Expanded Form → Sum of place valuesExample → 4567 = 4000 + 500 + 60 + 7
Frequency → Number of timesExample → A appears 3 times → Frequency = 3
Class 4 Maths – All Terms with Formulas & Examples (CBSE)
Expanded Form → Sum of place valuesExample → 45,678 = 40,000 + 5,000 + 600 + 70 + 8
Frequency → Number of times occursExample → A appears 4 times → Frequency = 4
Class 5 Maths – All Terms with Formulas & Examples (CBSE)
Expanded Form → Sum of place valuesExample → 3,45,678 = 3,00,000 + 40,000 + 5,000 + 600 + 70 + 8
Frequency → Number of timesExample → A appears 5 times → Frequency = 5
Class 6 Maths – All Terms with Formulas & Examples (CBSE)
Expanded Form → Sum of place valuesExample → 45,678 = 40,000 + 5,000 + 600 + 70 + 8
Class 7 Maths – All Terms with Formulas & Examples (CBSE)
Angle Sum Property → a + b + c = 180°Example → 60° + 70° + 50° = 180°
Circle Construction → Radius = constantExample → r = 5 cm
Line of Symmetry → Divides into equal halvesExample → Square has 4 lines
Class 8 Maths – All Terms with Formulas & Examples (CBSE)
Angle Sum → Sum of interior angles = 360°Example → 90° + 80° + 100° + 90° = 360°
Direct → y ∝ xExample → More items → more cost
Inverse → y ∝ 1/xExample → More workers → less time
Common Factor → ab + ac = a(b + c)Example → 2x + 4 = 2(x + 2)
Class 9 Maths – All Terms with Formulas & Examples (CBSE)
Angle Sum Property → a + b + c = 180°Example → 60° + 70° + 50° = 180°
Triangle Area → ½ × Base × HeightExample → ½ × 6 × 4 = 12 cm²
8. Place Value1 ten = 10 onesExample: 10 = 1 ten + 0 ones
a > b, a < b, a = bExample: 50 > 30
Place Value → Place Value = Digit × PlaceExample → In 3456, place value of 4 = 4 × 100 = 400
Expanded Form → Sum of place valuesExample → 5678 = 5000 + 600 + 70 + 8
Check → Dividend = Divisor × Quotient + RemainderExample → 45 ÷ 6 = 7 remainder 3→ 6 × 7 + 3 = 45 ✔
Factor → Number that divides exactlyExample → Factors of 12 = 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 12
Multiple → Product of numberExample → Multiples of 5 = 5, 10, 15, 20
Formula/Method → Common factors → Take highestExample → HCF of 12 & 18 = 6
Formula/Method → Common multiples → Take smallestExample → LCM of 4 & 6 = 12
Formula → Average = Total ÷ Number of itemsExample → (10 + 20 + 30) ÷ 3 = 20
Frequency → Number of times occursExample → If A comes 3 times → Frequency = 3
Formula → Probability = Favorable outcomes / Total outcomes
Euclid’s Division Lemma → a = bq + r
General Form → a₁x + b₁y + c₁ = 0a₂x + b₂y + c₂ = 0
Sum of n Terms → Sₙ = n/2 [2a + (n−1)d]
Formula → P(E) = Favorable outcomes / Total outcomes
Formula → n(A ∪ B) = n(A) + n(B) − n(A ∩ B)
Statement Form → If p then q
Factor → Exact divisor of a number
HCF = Highest common factor of given numbers
LCM = Lowest common multiple of given numbers
Mean = Sum of observations ÷ Number of observations
Angle Sum → Sum of interior angles = 360°
Common Factor → ab + ac = a(b + c)
Angle Sum Property → A + B + C = 180°
Formula → Probability = Favorable outcomes / Total outcomes
